message:
feat(cli): add `am beads` and `am agents detect` subcommands (br-27vd, br-2y14)

`am beads` provides ready/list/show/status commands that query the
local .beads/beads.db via beads_rust::storage::SqliteStorage.

`am agents detect` exposes coding_agent_session_search connector
probes through the CLI, reporting which coding agents are installed.

Includes 8 new tests (clap parsing + handler integration).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -298,6 +298,7 @@
 {"id":"br-1m6a.7","title":"Animated table effects on DB stats panel","description":"Showcase FrankenTUI table effects by animating the DB stats table in the live HUD.

CURRENT STATE
- The HUD DB stats panel is rendered with `ftui::widgets::table::Table` in `render_dashboard_frame()`.
- It currently has a static style (no effects), even though `TableTheme` supports deterministic effect animation.

TARGET EFFECTS
1) Subtle always-on "live" pulse
- Apply a `TableEffect::BreathingGlow` or `TableEffect::Pulse` to the DB stats header row (or the whole table) so the panel feels alive without being noisy.

2) Change-highlight sweep
- When a count value changes between ticks (e.g. messages count increases), apply a short-lived sweep/highlight effect to the affected row or cell.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Use real FrankenTUI table APIs (no custom resolvers):
  - `ftui_style::{TableTheme, TableEffectRule, TableEffectTarget, TableEffect}`
  - `Table::theme(theme)` + `Table::theme_phase(phase)`
- Phase must be deterministic:
  - Derive from dashboard tick count (e.g. `tick as f32 * 0.1`).
- To detect changes:
  - Store previous `DashboardDbStats` snapshot in `StartupDashboard`.
  - Compute a per-row change bitmask each tick.
  - Update the theme rules accordingly (or keep a small TTL counter per row).

SCOPE
- Keep effects tasteful and legible.
- If the animation makes CI/E2E flaky, provide a config gate:
  - `CONSOLE_EFFECTS_ENABLED` (default true in TTYs, false in CI).

TESTING
- Unit tests:
  - Theme construction contains expected effect rules.
  - Rendering does not panic at narrow widths.
  - Phase increments produce different cell attrs (basic sanity).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.140245099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:06:10.296473867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:06:10.296451735Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.7","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.140245099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.7","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:48.968104547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.8","title":"Live agent activity display in HUD","description":"Add a live agent activity section to the HUD so operators can see who is active without opening the Web UI.

CURRENT STATE
- HUD shows aggregate counts (projects/agents/messages/etc) but not a list of agents.

TARGET UX
- When terminal width permits, show a compact list of the most-recently-active agents:
  - `AgentName` (bold)
  - `program` (dim)
  - `last_active` (relative, e.g. `12s ago`)
- When narrow, collapse to a single summary line (agent count + most recent agent name).

DATA
- Extend `DashboardDbStats` (or add a sibling struct) with:
  - `agents_list: Vec<AgentSummary>`
- Query in `fetch_dashboard_db_stats()`:
  - `SELECT name, program, model, last_active_ts FROM agents ORDER BY last_active_ts DESC LIMIT 10`

RENDERING
- Add an "Agents" panel to `render_dashboard_frame()`:
  - Prefer a `Table` or simple `Paragraph` list depending on space.
  - Truncate safely to fit height.

PERF
- This runs on the dashboard refresh tick (currently 1200ms).
- Keep the query cheap (limit + index on last_active_ts if needed).

TESTING
- Unit tests for render behavior:
  - 0 agents -> panel omitted
  - 1/5/10 agents -> renders expected rows
  - narrow width -> graceful degradation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyPrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.241256939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T00:06:22.792073695Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:06:22.791992062Z","close_reason":"Implemented live agent activity display: AgentSummary struct, agents query in fetch_dashboard_db_stats, 4-column HUD layout with Agents panel, relative_time_short helper, narrow-width degradation. 7 unit tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.8","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.241256939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1m6a.9","title":"Web UI links section in startup banner","description":"Upgrade the existing "Web UI" section in the startup banner to include the full set of high-value links (Python parity + better discoverability).

CURRENT STATE
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/console.rs::render_startup_banner()` already renders a "Web UI" box.
- It currently prints only the main `/mail` URL + a single tip line.

TARGET LINKS (IN THE BANNER)
- Main UI: `http://{host}:{port}/mail`
- Unified inbox: `http://{host}:{port}/mail/unified-inbox`
- Archive guide: `http://{host}:{port}/mail/archive/guide`
- Template/pattern: `/mail/{project}/inbox/{agent}`

CLICKABLE LINKS (OPTIONAL)
- If terminal supports OSC-8 hyperlinks, wrap the first 3 URLs as clickable links.
  - Use `TerminalCapabilities::detect().osc8_hyperlinks` (available via the dashboard writer) or a conservative env override.
- Always include the raw URL text so logs remain useful when hyperlinks are unsupported.

SCOPE BOUNDARY
- This is banner-only (no server route changes).

TESTING
- Banner unit test asserts all URLs/pattern lines are present.
- If OSC-8 is emitted, test should assert the URL text is still present even after stripping OSC sequences.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.345197470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.258401005Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T23:39:30.252273375Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.9","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:40.345197470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-1m6a.9","depends_on_id":"br-1m6a.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T20:52:49.877162660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-1nh","title":"Share: scrub snapshot presets + secret regex","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Step 3: `scrub_snapshot` with legacy presets and secret redaction.

## Scope
- Presets: `standard`, `strict`, `archive` with exact flags (redact_body, drop_attachments, scrub_secrets, clear_ack_state, clear_recipients, clear_file_reservations, clear_agent_links).
- Secret regex patterns (GH tokens, slack, sk‑, bearer, JWT, etc.) replaced with `[REDACTED]`.
- Attachment metadata scrub: remove keys (download_url, headers, authorization, signed_url, bearer_token).
- Body redaction for strict: replace with `"[Message body redacted]"`.
- Return `ScrubSummary` with counts (agents_total, ack_flags_cleared, recipients_cleared, etc.).

## Tests
- Unit tests for each preset and regex redaction.
- Integration tests on seeded snapshot DB with attachments and secrets.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Save scrub summary JSON and diffs under `tests/artifacts/share/scrub/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Preset behavior matches legacy exactly (counts + outputs).
2. Secret patterns are redacted without altering non‑secrets.
3. Attachments scrubbed per key list with correct counts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:20.358844765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.704668583Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.704649176Z","close_reason":"Implemented scrub_snapshot presets + secret regex + attachment metadata scrubbing; conformance fixtures pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1nh","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:23.418674217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-1r9v","title":"Console layout config: HUD sizing + persistence","description":"Add interactive console layout configuration allowing users to control HUD size/position vs scrolling log output, with automatic persistence.

USER NEED:
- Configure how much screen real estate the static HUD occupies vs the scrolling log.
  - Examples: bottom 20%, bottom 50%.
- Do this interactively and have settings remembered.

DEFAULTS (must be great out-of-the-box):
- Rich HUD starts automatically in a real TTY when `LOG_RICH_ENABLED=true` (default).
- Default HUD size is ~33% of terminal height, anchored bottom.

CONFIGURABLE SETTINGS (env vars + persisted user config):
1) `CONSOLE_UI_HEIGHT_PERCENT`: u8 (10-80, default 33)
   - Maps to: `ui_height = (term_height * pct / 100).max(8).min(term_height - 2)`

2) `CONSOLE_UI_ANCHOR`: enum {bottom, top} (default bottom)
   - Maps to `ftui::UiAnchor`

3) `CONSOLE_UI_AUTO_SIZE`: bool (default false)
   - When true: use `ScreenMode::InlineAuto { min_height, max_height }` instead of fixed.

4) `CONSOLE_AUTO_SAVE`: bool (default true)
   - When true, interactive adjustments persist immediately.

PERSISTENCE FILE:
- `~/.config/agent-mail/console.toml`
  - Priority: env vars > console.toml > defaults
  - Saving should preserve stable formatting (don’t churn on every save).

INTERACTIVE ADJUSTMENT:
- HTTP server mode only (stdio mode uses stdin for MCP protocol).
- Spawn a lightweight `/dev/tty` (or stdin) reader thread when running in a real TTY:
  - Up / '+': increase `ui_height_percent` by 5 (max 80)
  - Down / '-': decrease `ui_height_percent` by 5 (min 10)
  - 't'/'b': toggle anchor top/bottom
  - 'a': toggle auto-size
  - 's': force-save settings
  - 'q': disable interactive adjustments for this run
- HUD should display a subtle, dim hint line when interactive mode is active:
  - `+/- resize | t/b anchor | a autosize | s save`

INTEGRATION NOTES:
- Left/right splits ("left 30%") require AltScreen + an internal log viewer.
  - That’s implemented separately in `br-1m6a.19`.

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Add config fields to `mcp_agent_mail_core::Config` + `from_env()` parsing.
- Add load/save for `console.toml` (no new global config system; just this file).
- Modify `StartupDashboard::maybe_start()` to use configured values instead of hardcoded 1/3.

TESTING:
- Config loading priority (env > file > defaults)
- `ui_height` calculation + clamp behavior
- Anchor parsing fallback
- Save/load roundtrip for console.toml
- Interactive key handling logic (unit test the state machine; no real tty required)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Users can resize HUD interactively in HTTP mode and it persists.
- Defaults remain enabled and sane with zero config.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:18:52.502187876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:38:30.839103222Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:38:30.839080589Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of br-1m6a.19 (merged console layout plan there).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"br-27m","title":"Share: search indexes + materialized views + perf indexes","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 4–6: FTS5 search indexes, materialized views, and performance indexes for static viewer.

## Scope
- **build_search_indexes**: create `fts_messages` virtual table; populate with subject/body/importance/project/thread_key/created_ts; optimize.
- **build_materialized_views**: create `message_overview_mv`, `attachments_by_message_mv`, `fts_search_overview_mv` (if FTS enabled).
- **create_performance_indexes**: add lowercase columns and covering indexes for fast viewer filtering.

## Tests
- Unit tests for FTS presence/absence path.
- Integration tests verifying view row counts and index existence.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store schema dumps under `tests/artifacts/share/indexes/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. FTS and MV tables match legacy schemas and row counts.
2. Performance indexes created without errors, idempotent.
3. Viewer search works against generated bundle DB.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:32.931924389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:00:45.523981969Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:00:45.523958655Z","close_reason":"Validated share index/view parity; ensured message_recipients table exists","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-27m","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:36.800683710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-28u","title":"Share: chunk database (4MiB chunks)","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Step 9: database chunking for large bundles.

## Scope
- Default threshold: 20MiB; chunk size 4MiB.
- Split DB file into sequential chunks with deterministic naming/order.
- Update manifest to reference chunks and original size.

## Tests
- Integration test with synthetic DB large enough to trigger chunking.
- Verify chunk count, sizes, and reassembly hash match original.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store chunk metadata under `tests/artifacts/share/chunking/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Chunking triggers only above threshold and produces correct chunk sizes.
2. Reassembly yields byte‑identical DB.
3. Manifest updated with chunk metadata.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:09.803257199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:49:54.658358572Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:49:54.658340589Z","close_reason":"Chunking already implemented in bundle.rs with tests; verified chunk_* tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-28u","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:14.635619585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2di","title":"CLI: share preview parity (hotkeys + exit codes)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share preview` command with legacy server behavior and hotkeys.

## Scope
- Defaults: host `127.0.0.1`, port `9000`, `--open-browser` default false.
- Serve static bundle with no-cache headers.
- Hotkeys: `r` reload, `d` deploy request, `q` quit.
- Exit code **42** when deploy requested via `d`.

## Tests
- Integration test with temp bundle; verify server responds and hotkeys trigger.
- Assert exit code 42 on deploy request.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store preview server logs under `tests/artifacts/cli/share_preview/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share preview matches legacy defaults and hotkey behavior.
2. Exit codes match legacy (42 on deploy request).
3. No-cache headers are set on responses.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralDog","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:04.316431054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:50:39.558932277Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T11:50:39.558909405Z","close_reason":"Implemented share preview server parity: /__preview__/status + no-cache headers, hotkeys r/d/q with exit code 42 on deploy; added integration test + crossterm","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2di","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:44.188268904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2di","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:08.106147106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei","title":"MCP Agent Mail Rust: 100% feature parity + conformance + benchmarks","description":"## Objective
Deliver a Rust port of legacy `mcp_agent_mail` with **100% behavior parity**, including MCP tools/resources, HTTP transport, CLI, storage/git archive, mail UI, share/export pipeline, and background workers. The port must use the local crates (`/dp/fastmcp_rust`, `/dp/sqlmodel_rust`, `/dp/asupersync`, `/dp/frankentui`, `/dp/beads_rust`, `/dp/coding_agent_session_search`) and avoid tokio where asupersync is the intended runtime.

## Scope (Non‑Negotiable)
- **Parity with legacy Python** as specified in `EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md` and verified by conformance fixtures.
- **MCP server**: tools/resources, tool filtering, TOON output format, instrumentation, notifications, LLM integration.
- **HTTP server**: streamable HTTP transport, auth/JWT/RBAC, rate limiting, logging, background workers, mail UI.
- **CLI**: all Typer-equivalent commands + flags + exit codes + outputs (human + JSON).
- **Storage**: git archive artifacts, locks, attachments, notifications signals.
- **Share/Export**: full static bundle pipeline + crypto + deterministic packaging.
- **Quality gates**: conformance + unit/integration + E2E + benchmarks with artifacts.

## Deliverables
- **Conformance harness** covering all tools/resources (fixture-based).
- **E2E suite** (HTTP/CLI/archive/share/guard) with detailed artifacts.
- **Bench suite** with budgets and regression checks.
- **Feature parity report** (`FEATURE_PARITY.md`) kept accurate.

## Tests
- Conformance tests for all tools/resources + TOON + LLM (stubbed).
- Unit/integration tests per subsystem (storage, DB, HTTP, CLI, share).
- E2E scripts with deterministic fixtures.
- Benchmark smoke + budgets.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Standardized artifacts under `tests/artifacts/...` for all failure modes.
- JSON diff outputs for conformance/CLI/HTTP comparisons.
- Bench summaries + flamegraphs where applicable.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. All behavior and requirements described above are implemented with legacy parity.
2. Unit/integration tests cover the described cases with deterministic outputs and logged artifacts.
3. Any referenced E2E coverage passes and captures the expected logs/artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"CoralDog","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:05:51.223489285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:28:52.478705773Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T20:28:52.478683021Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed. 100% feature parity achieved: 23 tools, 23+ resources, 1005+ workspace tests, 15+ E2E suites (archive, guard, HTTP, stdio, CLI, share, macros, etc.), full conformance. FEATURE_PARITY.md shows all items Verified.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"br-2ei","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deep review fixes landed: storage archive path traversal validation (agent names + extra_paths) w/ tests; tooling schemas determinism (HashMap->BTreeMap) + base-segment percent-decoding; removed duplicate tool_metrics shutdown call; CLI integration tests no longer mutate env vars (Rust 2024 set_var unsafety).  {"id":"br-2yw0","title":"Guard parity: unit + integration tests","description":"## Objective
Add unit/integration tests for guard behavior beyond the E2E repo test.

## Scope
- Path matching (pathspec + fnmatch fallback).
- Pre‑commit scanning (staged files, rename handling).
- Pre‑push scanning (commit range).
- Gate on `WORKTREES_ENABLED`/`GIT_IDENTITY_ENABLED` per legacy guard.py.

## Tests
- Unit tests for pathspec matches and denylist/allowlist behavior.
- Integration tests using temp git repo with staged changes and renames.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store guard decision logs under `tests/artifacts/guard/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Guard behavior matches legacy for staged/renamed files and gate flags.
2.  {"id":"br-36w","title":"Config parity: .env loading + env var coverage","description":"## Objective
Match legacy configuration behavior, including `.env` loading and full env var coverage.

## Scope
- `.env` loading behavior (python‑decouple parity):
  - if .env missing → empty repository fallback; env vars only.
  - canonical env var set only if missing when alias present (LLM bridge).
- Ensure all env vars from spec are supported:
  - WORKTREES_ENABLED + GIT_IDENTITY_ENABLED gate
  - ALLOW_ABSOLUTE_ATTACHMENT_PATHS
  - TOOLS_LOG_ENABLED, LOG_RICH_ENABLED
  - NOTIFICATIONS_* defaults
  - HTTP_* (CORS/JWT/RBAC/rate limit)
  - LLM_* defaults
- CSV parsing for list settings (roles, tools, clusters).
- Match default values exactly.

## Tests
- Unit tests for .env presence/absence handling.
- Table‑driven env parsing tests for bools/ints/CSV lists.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store parsed config snapshots under `tests/artifacts/config/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Rust config matches legacy defaults and parsing semantics.
2. `.env` loading behavior matches python‑decouple (graceful missing file).
3. All env variables from spec are supported and tested.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:09.094825172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:02:50.832107234Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:02:50.832088128Z","close_reason":"Implemented .env loading + env var coverage + tests; checks blocked by sqlmodel-sqlite duplicate defs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-36w","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:18:12.895192349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-3gs","title":"Share: sign manifest + encrypt bundle + deterministic ZIP","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Steps 13–15: Ed25519 signing, age encryption, and deterministic ZIP packaging.

## Scope
- **sign_manifest**: sign manifest.json with Ed25519 seed; optionally output public key.
- **encrypt_bundle**: age encryption when recipients provided; output `.age` file; retain clear bundle if required by legacy.
- **package_directory_as_zip**: deterministic ZIP (stable ordering, timestamps, compression settings).

## Tests
- Unit tests for signature verification and age encrypt/decrypt.
- Integration tests for deterministic ZIP hash.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store signatures, public keys, and zip hashes under `tests/artifacts/share/crypto/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Manifest signatures validate correctly with provided public key.
2. Encryption outputs readable bundles via `share decrypt`.
3. ZIP packaging is deterministic (stable hash across runs).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CopperOwl","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:36.383969609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.811535263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T06:53:02.811510687Z","close_reason":"Verified sign/encrypt/zip implementation via mcp-agent-mail-share tests (age roundtrip + deterministic zip)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3gs","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:41.980704900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"br-3gs","author":"CopperOwl","text":"Claimed br-3gs.

- Run gates (cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-share) and close br-3gs.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:38:29Z"}]}
